title: 第 45 節
en: ever you do not understand of your Self3, come and teh1 me.
' They looked in the water-pan. Then Pra^apati said to them : " What do you see 1 "
' They said : " We both see the Self thus altogether, a picture even to the very hairs and nails."' i.
' Pra^apati said to them : " After you have adorned yourselves, have put on your best clothes and cleaned yourselves, look again into the water-pan."
' They, after having adorned themselves, having put on their best clothes, and cleaned themselves, looked into the water-pan.

1 The commentator explains this rightly. Pra^apati means the person that is seen in the eye, that is, the real agent of seeing, who is seen by sages even with their eyes shut. His pupils, how ever, misunderstand him. They think of the person that is seen, not of the person that sees. The person seen in the eye is to them the small figure imaged in the eye, and they go on therefore to ask, whether the image in the water or in a mirror is not the Self.

2 The commentators are at great pains to explain that Pra^apati told no falsehood. He meant by purusha the personal element in the highest sense, and it was not his fault that his pupils took purusha for man or body.
3 I take atmana/t as a genitive, governed by yad, not as an accusative plural.
' Prap'apati said : " What do you see V" 2.
' They said : " Just as we are, well adorned, with our best clothes and clean, thus we are both there, Sir, well adorned, with our best clothes and clean."
'Pra^apati said: "That is the Self, this is the immortal, the fearless, this is Brahman."
' Then both went away satisfied in their hearts.

' And Prae/apati looking after them, said : " They both go away without having perceived and without having known the Self, and whoever of these two1, whether Devas or Asuras, will follow this doctrine (upanishad), will perish."

' Now Viro&ana satisfied in his heart went to the Asuras and preached that doctrine to them, that the Self (the body) alone is to be worshipped, that the Self (the body) alone is to be served, and that he who worships the Self and serves the Self, gains both worlds, this and the next.' 4.

' Therefore they call even now a man who does not give alms here, who has no faith, and offers no sacri fices, an Asura, for this is the doctrine (upanishad) of the Asuras. They deck out the body of the dead with perfumes, flowers, and fine raiment by way of orna ment, and think they will thus conquer that world.' 5.
' But Indra, before he had returned to the Devas, saw this difficulty. As this Self (the shadow in the water)2 is well adorned when the body is well 1 The commentator reads yatare for yata/«.

2 The commentator remarks that though both Indra and Viro&ana had mistaken the true import of what Pra^apati said, yet while Viro&ana took the body to be the Self, Indra thought that the Self was the shadow of the body.

PHILOSOPHY AND RELIGION. 323 adorned, well dressed when the body is well dressed, well cleaned if the body is well cleaned, that Self will also be blind if the body is blind, lame if the body is lame1, crippled if the body is crippled, and will perish in fact as soon as the body perishes. Therefore I see no good in this (doctrine)/ i.

' Taking fuel in his hand he came again as a pupil to Pra^apati. Prae/apati said to him : " Maghavat (Indra), as you went away with Viro&aiia, satisfied in your heart, for what purpose did you come back \ "

' He said : " Sir, as this Self (the shadow) is well adorned when the body is well adorned, well dressed when the body is well dressed, well cleaned if the body is well cleaned, that Self will also be blind if the body is blind, lame if the body is lame, crippled if the body is crippled, and will perish in fact as soon as the body perishes. Therefore I see no good in this (doctrine)/3' 2.
'"So it is indeed, Maghavat/' replied Pra^apati; " but I shall explain him (the true Self) further to you. Live with me another thirty-two years."
' He lived with him another thirty-two years, and then Pra</apati said : 3.
TENTH '"He who moves about happy in dreams, he is the Self, this is the immortal, the fearless, this is Brahman."

' Then Indra went away satisfied in his heart. But before he had returned to the Devas, he saw this difficulty. Now although it is true that that Self is 1 Srama, lame, is explained by the commentator as one-eyed ekanetra.

not blind even if the body is blind, nor lame, if the body is lame, though it is true that that Self is not rendered faulty by the faults of it (the body), nor struck when it (the body) is struck, nor lamed when it is lamed, yet it is as if they struck him (the Self) in dreams, as if they drove him away. He becomes even conscious, as it were, of pain, and sheds tears. Therefore I see no good in this/ i.

' Taking fuel in his hands, he went again as a pupil to Pra^apati. Pra</apati said to him : " Maghavat, as you went away satisfied in your heart, for what purpose did you come back V ' He said : " Sir, although it is true that that Self is not blind even if the body is blind, nor lame if the body is lame, though it is true that that Self is not rendered faulty by the faults of it (the body), nor struck when it (the body) is struck, nor lamed when it is lamed, yet it is as if they struck him (the Self) in dreams, as if they drove him away. He becomes even conscious, as it were of pain, and sheds tears. Therefore I see no good in this.'" i.
' " So it is indeed, Maghavat," replied Pra^apati ; " but I shall explain him (the true Self) further to you. Live with me another thirty-two years."
'He lived with him another thirty-two years. Then Pra^apati said: 4.
' " When a man being asleep, reposing, and at perfect rest l, sees no dreams, that is the Self, this is the immortal, the fearless, this is Brahman/'
' Then Indra went awav satisfied in his heart. But 1 See A7iandogya Upanishad, VIII, 6, 3.
zh: ……凡是你對自身（Self³）有所不解之處，儘管前來告訴我¹。
他們往水盆裡看。普拉賈帕提便對他們說：「你們看見了什麼？」
「他們說：『我們兩人都如此全然見到自我，甚至連毛髮與指甲都像一幅畫。』」¹
'普拉賈帕提對他們說：「你們裝扮好、穿上最好的衣服並潔淨自身之後，再往水盆裡看。」
他們裝扮完畢，穿上最好的衣服，並潔淨自身之後，便朝水盆中觀看。
1 註釋者正確地解釋了這一點。生主（Prajāpati）意指眼中所見之人，亦即觀看的真實主體，即使聖人閉上雙眼也能看見祂。然而，祂的弟子們誤解了祂。他們想到的是被看見之人，而非觀看之人。對他們而言，眼中所見之人是映在眼睛裡的小小形像，因此他們繼續追問：水中或鏡中的影像，難道不正是自我（Self）嗎？
註釋家們費盡心力解釋，說生主（Prajāpati）並未說謊。他以 *puruṣa* 意指最高意義上的人格要素，而他的弟子們將 *puruṣa* 理解為「人」或「身體」，這並非他的過錯。
3 我將 *ātmanaḥ* 視為受 *yad* 管轄的所有格，而非受格複數。
> 「生主（Prajāpati）問道：『你看見了什麼？』」
他們說：「正如我們此刻盛裝、穿著最好的衣服且潔淨，先生，我們在那邊也同樣盛裝、穿著最好的衣服且潔淨。」
『普拉賈帕提說：「那就是自我，這是不朽者，無畏者，這是梵。」』
『於是兩人心滿意足地離去。』
' 生主（Prajāpati）注視著他們，說道：「這兩者都未能領悟、未能認識自我（Self）便離去了；無論是提婆（Devas）或阿修羅（Asuras），凡追隨此教義（upanishad）者，都將滅亡。」
> 『如今，維羅恰納心中滿足，便前往阿修羅處，向他們宣講此教義：唯有自我（身體）值得崇拜，唯有自我（身體）值得侍奉；凡崇拜自我、侍奉自我者，便能獲得此世與來世兩個世界。』⁴
> 「因此他們至今仍稱呼那在此不施捨、無信仰、不獻祭的人為阿修羅（Asura），因為這就是阿修羅的教義（upanishad）。他們以香料、鮮花與華服裝飾死者遺體作為妝點，並以為如此便能征服那個世界。」
> 「然而，因陀羅（Indra）在返回提婆（Devas）之前，便看出了此一難題。由於這個自我（水中的倒影）² 在身體裝扮得宜時亦隨之華美¹，……」
2 註釋者指出，雖然因陀羅（Indra）與維羅恰那（Virocana）都誤解了生主（Prajāpati）所說的真實意涵，然而維羅恰那將身體視為自我（Self），因陀羅則認為自我是身體的影子。
裝飾華麗，身體裝飾得當，自我便裝飾得當；身體潔淨，自我便潔淨；若身體盲目，自我亦盲目；若身體跛足，自我亦跛足¹；若身體殘缺，自我亦殘缺；事實上，身體一旦消亡，自我也隨之消亡。因此，我在此（教義）中看不出任何益處。
'他手持薪柴，再次以弟子之身來到生主面前。生主對他說：「摩伽梵（因陀羅），你與維羅闇那離去時，心中已得滿足，為何又返回？」
> 他說：「先生，正如這個自我（陰影）在身體裝飾華美時也裝飾華美，身體衣著整齊時也衣著整齊，身體潔淨時也潔淨；同樣地，若身體失明，這個自我也將失明；若身體跛足，自我也跛足；若身體殘廢，自我也殘廢；而一旦身體毀滅，這個自我實際上也會隨之毀滅。因此，我看不出此（教義）有何益處。」
「確實如此，摩伽梵（Maghavat），」生主（Prajāpati）答道，「但我將進一步向你解說他（真實的自我）。再與我同住三十二年。」
『他又與祂同住了三十二年，隨後生主（Prajāpati）說：3.』
> 「那在夢中快樂遊走者，即是自我，此乃不朽者、無畏者，此即梵。」
> 「然後因陀羅心中滿意地離去。但在他回到提婆（Devas）之前，他看見了這個困難。如今，雖然那個自我確實是跛足的（1 Srama，註釋者解釋為獨眼 ekanetra），……」
> 即使身體是盲的，自我（Self）也不盲；即使身體是跛的，自我也不跛。誠然，那個自我不會因身體的過失而有缺陷，身體被打時它不會被打，身體被弄跛時它也不會被弄跛；然而，在夢中，它卻彷彿被打、彷彿被驅逐。它甚至彷彿意識到痛苦，並流下眼淚。因此，我認為這並無益處。

「他手持薪柴，再次以弟子之身前往拜見缽羅闍缽底（Pra^apati）。缽羅闍缽底對他說：『摩伽梵（Maghavat），你既已心滿意足地離去，為何又回來？』他答道：『尊者，雖然確實那自我（Self）不會因身體盲目而盲目，不會因身體跛足而跛足；雖然確實那自我不會因身體的過失而有過失，不會因身體受擊而受擊，也不會因身體跛足而跛足——然而在夢中，他們彷彿擊打他（自我），彷彿驅逐他。他甚至彷彿意識到痛苦，流下淚水。因此，我認為此事並無益處。』」
「確實如此，摩伽梵（Maghavat），」生主（Prajāpati）答道；「但我將進一步向你解說他（真實的自我）。再與我同住三十二年吧。」
「他又與他同住了三十二年。然後生主說：」
「當一個人沉睡、安臥、處於完全靜止，且不作夢時，那就是『自我』（Self），這是不朽者、無畏者，這就是『梵』（Brahman）。」
' 於是因陀羅心滿意足地離去。¹ ¹ 參見《阿闍多迦奧義書》（A7iandogya Upanishad）VIII, 6, 3。
